headline: Nasdaq-100 H1 gains concentrated in just 10 stocks
detail: This confirms breadth is extremely thin inside the index. Your SMH and QQQ exposure is fine as long as those 10 names hold, but a rotation or selloff in mega-cap tech drags the whole index with almost no cushion from the other 90 names. For a margin trader concentrated in tech/chips, thin breadth means the floor can drop fast with little warning.
rec: No specific trade triggered, but this is a reason to keep your QQQ stop at $670 honest and not add new tech exposure until breadth confirms - don't chase the leaders at these levels.

headline: ADP private payrolls +98K, below expectations
detail: Soft jobs data suggests the labor market is cooling, which could give the Fed cover to cut rates sooner - historically a tailwind for growth/tech names like your holdings. However, if the market reads it as an economic slowdown signal, risk-off can hit high-beta names like SOXL and SPAL hardest and fastest, especially on margin.
rec: No action today - watch how the market interprets this into the close. A rally in QQQ/SMH on rate-cut hopes would be a better entry environment; a selloff would pressure your leveraged positions first.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce flagged as buys, Armageddon fears overdone
detail: An analyst call that fear around enterprise software has been excessive - relevant because CRM appears in your scanner setups (entry $158.46-158.66, stop $155.99) and the analyst view provides a fundamental reason to look at it. This is the kind of catalyst that can validate a mechanical scanner setup rather than just chasing price.
rec: CRM is worth watching at scanner entry $158.46-158.66 with stop $155.99 if price pulls back there - analyst catalyst plus a grade-C scanner setup means keep size small and wait for the level, don't chase.

headline: Oil prices fall amid U.S.-Iran talks uncertainty
detail: Falling oil is generally a mild positive for tech and chip names since it reduces input costs and keeps inflation expectations contained, supporting the rate-cut narrative. Not a direct driver for your holdings but reinforces the macro backdrop that has been supportive of QQQ and SMH this year.
rec: No trade here - this is background macro support for your existing holds, not a reason to add or trim anything specific today.
